attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 51 AUC answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AUC casebundles in this batch (n=51)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-URO-01-00215 — SEQ is provisional; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-URO-01 bucket. Source note ID AUC-015 recorded only here.

Age (~68) and older_adult band inferred from a 6-month history of progressive obstructive urinary symptoms (hesitancy, poor stream, nocturia) on a benign-feeling enlarged prostate; no explicit age in the source — confirm on clinician review.
Male sex inferred from prostate / BPH context — confirm on clinician review.
Geographic context not inferable from 'private residence' — omitted.

Telehealth reprojection applied: source is an in-person after-hours home visit where the clinician palpated/percussed the bladder, performed a DRE, did a bedside bladder ultrasound and inserted a 16Fr Foley catheter draining 1200 mL. These clinician-only findings and the procedure are sealed in 10/11 (physical_sign / investigation_result) with telehealth_limits; the AI-appropriate action is recognise + escalate for urgent same-day catheterisation.

Measured vitals (BP 160/95, HR 110, Temp 37.0, RR 20, SpO2 99%, Wt 84 kg) were clinician-obtained with no stated home device, so they are sealed in 11 as physical_sign / cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth rather than placed in 01.objective_data_offered.

Baseline triage set to T4 (ED today for catheterisation), not T5 — acute retention needs same-day decompression but is not an immediate call-000 emergency unless self-transport is impossible or infected-obstruction/collapse features develop (encoded as escalation edges). Under-triage weighting applied conservatively.

No dose synthesised: the source names 'alpha-blocker therapy' and future prostate-shrinking medication without doses; tamsulosin/finasteride recorded without dose_route_frequency per the no-invented-dose rule (GP-initiated at follow-up).
SNOMED/ICD-10-AM/AMT codes are candidates only (unverified_pending_terminology_receipt); ICD-10-AM R33.8 vs R33 and the tamsulosin AMT code to be confirmed against the terminology server.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
